name: Michigan Pasties
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Shredded cheddar cheese, finely diced
1 cup Cooked chicken, shredded (or substitute cooked beef or turkey)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
2. Add the vegetable shortening to the dry ingredients. Using a pastry cutter or your fingers, work the shortening into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Beat the eggs in a small bowl. Gradually add the cold water to the beaten eggs, stirring constantly to prevent curdling.
4. Pour the egg and water mixture over the flour mixture. Stir with a fork until the dough begins to come together.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide into two equal portions.
6. Shape one portion of dough into a disk and wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to overnight.
7. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough into a large circle or rectangle, about 1/8 inch thick.
9. Place the rolled-out dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
10. In a bowl, combine shredded cheddar cheese, finely chopped onion, shredded chicken (or substitute), Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, and red pepper flakes (if using).
11. Spoon the filling onto the center of the dough, leaving about 2 inches of overhang on all sides.
12. Fold the edges of the dough up and over the filling to create a pasty shell. Seal the edges by pressing them together with your fingertips.
13. Transfer the pasties to a baking sheet and bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown.
14. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack before serving.
